summary: Tested Stripe Subscription + Referral Credit system. All 16 backend pytest tests passed (subscriptions: status, checkout cadet/operator/invalid, cancel, auth gates; referrals: my-code format TF-XXXXXX, idempotency, apply invalid/own code, credits, auth gates). Frontend Pricing page tested: all 4 tier cards render with proper CTAs, RECRUIT CTA is no-op, CADET CTA when unauthenticated redirects to /login, CADET CTA when logged in as admin successfully redirects to real Stripe checkout (checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_...), annual billing toggle applies 20% discount ($19 -> $15).
critical_code_review_comments: subscriptions.py cancel endpoint downgrades user immediately by setting tier='free' and agent_limit=3, contradicting the response message 'downgrades to Recruit at period end'. Either rename to immediate cancel or defer the user-tier update until period end.

activate_subscription_from_payment marks referral credit used by querying credits.find_one({user_id, used:False}) when credit_id is missing — could mark the wrong credit if multiple credits exist. Prefer matching by stored credit_id which is already in metadata.
TIERS are duplicated server-side (subscriptions.py) vs frontend (Pricing.jsx). Acceptable since server validates, but consider exposing a GET /api/subscriptions/tiers to avoid drift.
Pricing.jsx final price calc using max(1.00, price - discount) silently floors instead of communicating the floor to the user; cosmetic only.
seed_data_creation: Created one fresh user per test run via /api/auth/register (TEST_referee_<uuid>@example.com) for free-tier and apply-referral tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Subscription/referral endpoints all live under /api/subscriptions/* and /api/referrals/*. Stripe is in test mode (sk_test_emergent) and creates real checkout sessions at checkout.stripe.com — verified via redirect. Admin user has a persistent referral code generated. To test referral apply success path end-to-end, register a new user and POST /api/referrals/apply with admin's code; this will create $10 credit for both parties (verified logic, not tested in this run to avoid mutating admin's credit state).
